import os
import json
import argparse
import torch
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader
from torchvision import transforms
from tqdm import tqdm

from model import FMoWModel
from dataset import FMoWTestDataset

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument('--data_path', type=str, required=True)
    parser.add_argument('--checkpoint', type=str, required=True)
    parser.add_argument('--output', type=str, required=True)
    parser.add_argument('--batch_size', type=int, default=128)
    parser.add_argument('--model_name', type=str, default='openai/clip-vit-large-patch14')
    args = parser.parse_args()

    device = torch.device('cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu')
    
    val_transform = transforms.Compose([
        transforms.Resize(224),
        transforms.CenterCrop(224),
        transforms.ToTensor(),
        transforms.Normalize(mean=[0.48145466, 0.4578275, 0.40821073], 
                             std=[0.26862954, 0.26130258, 0.27577711])
    ])
    
    dataset = FMoWTestDataset(args.data_path, transform=val_transform)
    loader = DataLoader(dataset, batch_size=args.batch_size, shuffle=False, num_workers=8, pin_memory=True)
    
    model = FMoWModel(model_name=args.model_name, num_classes=62)
    model.load_state_dict(torch.load(args.checkpoint, map_location='cpu'))
    model.to(device)
    model.eval()
    
    all_preds = []
    
    with torch.no_grad():
        for images, _ in tqdm(loader, desc="Predicting"):
            images = images.to(device)
            outputs = model(images)
            preds = outputs.argmax(dim=1)
            all_preds.extend(preds.cpu().tolist())
            
    with open(args.output, 'w') as f:
        json.dump({"predictions": all_preds}, f)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
